Excellent — this is a **trust & audit clarity feature**, and it absolutely deserves its **own phase**.
What you’re describing is essentially a **human-readable payment timeline**, attached to every financial record.

Let’s formalize it cleanly.

---

# Phase 8: Payment History & Audit Notes (Trust & Transparency)

**Goal:**
Make every payment **self-explanatory**, so users never ask:

> “How was this paid?” or “Why does this show as completed?”

---

## 1. Core UX Concept

Every **income day** and every **expense** should have:

* A **read-only Payment History panel**
* Automatically generated notes
* Chronological, append-only entries

⚠️ Users do **not manually edit** these notes — the system writes them.

---

## 2. Where This Appears in the UI

### Trigger

* Clicking on:

  * A daily collection card
  * An expense row
  * A settlement entry

### UI Panel (Side drawer or modal)

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Payment History                          │
├──────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ March 5, 2026                            │
│ Expected: $100                           │
│                                          │
│ • Collected $50                          │
│   → Partial payment                      │
│   → Paid on Mar 5, 2026                  │
│                                          │
│ • Settled $50                            │
│   → Settlement from April               │
│   → Settled on Apr 2, 2026               │
│                                          │
│ Status: Fully settled                    │
└──────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## 3. Payment Note Types (System-Generated)

### Income Payment Notes

* **Initial collection**

  * “Collected $X on [date]”
  * If partial → “Marked as partial”

* **Settlement**

  * “Settled $X on [date]”
  * “Applied from [month]”

### Expense Payment Notes

* **Expense created**

  * “Expense of $X created for Week N”

* **Expense payment**

  * “Paid $X on [date]”
  * Partial or full automatically inferred

---

## 4. Data Model Extension (Minimal & Powerful)

### payment_events (new table)

| Field        | Type      | Notes                       |
| ------------ | --------- | --------------------------- |
| id           | UUID      |                             |
| entity_type  | ENUM      | DAILY_COLLECTION / EXPENSE  |
| entity_id    | UUID      |                             |
| event_type   | ENUM      | COLLECTED / SETTLED / PAID  |
| amount       | DECIMAL   |                             |
| event_date   | DATE      |                             |
| reference_id | UUID      | settlement_id or payment_id |
| created_at   | TIMESTAMP |                             |

✔ Append-only
✔ Immutable
✔ Perfect audit trail

---

## 5. How Notes Are Generated (System Rules)

* Notes are **derived from payment_events**
* UI never stores raw text
* Language is standardized (no ambiguity)

### Example Generation Logic

```
if event_type == COLLECTED and amount < expected:
  "Collected $X — partial payment"

if event_type == SETTLED:
  "Settled $X from future payment"
```

---

## 6. Updating Notes Over Time (Your Key Requirement)

### Scenario

* March 5 → Partial $50
* April 2 → Settlement $50

### Resulting History

```
• Collected $50 on Mar 5, 2026 (Partial)
• Settled $50 on Apr 2, 2026
```

✔ Original note preserved
✔ Settlement appended
✔ Status auto-updates to “Fully settled”

---

## 7. Visual Status Indicators

* 🟡 Partial
* 🟢 Fully settled
* 🔴 Outstanding
* 🔵 Expense paid

Status is **derived**, never stored.

---

## 8. Rules (Very Important)

* Payment history is:

  * Read-only
  * Append-only
  * Auto-generated

* No deletion

* No editing

* No manual notes for payments

---

## 9. Why This Phase Matters

✔ Eliminates confusion
✔ Builds trust
✔ Enables exports & reports
✔ Makes bugs obvious
✔ Prepares for multi-user / accountant view

---

## 10. Phase 8 Completion Criteria

Phase 8 is complete when:

* Every payment shows a clear timeline
* Partial → settled flows are obvious
* Users never ask “how was this paid?”
* No financial record exists without a history view

---

## Guiding Rule (Carry Forward)

> **Money without history is a bug.**

---
